---
title: "Finite State Transducers with Pynini"
subtitle: "A sample morphological analyzer for English"
author: "Oren Bochman"
date: "2024-06-01"
---


What I wanted was a quick tutorial - but wat i found on the web did not work. Gemini got stuck in endeless API archology.

```{bash}
!uv pip install panini graphviz
```




Define the FST

We’ll create an FST that analyzes the word “running” into its root and suffix: “run+ing”.


```{python}
from __future__ import division

from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
import pywrapfst
from IPython.display import Image, display

import pynini
# Specific tools still available at the top level
from pynini import compose, invert, isomorphic, NO_STATE_ID
from pynini import prune, shortestdistance, shortestpath, string_map
from pynini import synchronize, union

def byte_symbol_table(chars: str):
    """
    Pynini's default labels are byte integers.
    This table makes DOT/Graphviz show readable symbols.
    """
    syms = pywrapfst.SymbolTable()
    syms.add_symbol("<eps>", 0)

    for ch in sorted(set(chars)):
        syms.add_symbol(ch, ord(ch))

    return syms

```

```{python}
#| label: fst-baa
#| echo: true

sheep = pynini.accep("b",token_type="utf8") + pynini.accep("a",token_type="utf8").plus
sheep.optimize() 

symbols = byte_symbol_table("ba")
dot = sheep.draw("sheep.dot", portrait=True, isymbols=symbols, osymbols=symbols)
dot
```

``` {python}    

o_to_a = pynini.cross("o", "a")
one_or_more_o_to_a = o_to_a + pynini.closure(o_to_a)

rule = pynini.cross("m", "b") + one_or_more_o_to_a + pynini.cross("?", "!")

for s in ["mo?", "moo?", "mooo?"]:
    print(s, "->", pynini.shortestpath(s @ rule).string())
```


```{python}
#| label: fst-running-analyzer
#| echo: true



def x(inp: str, out: str):
    """One-symbol transducer."""
    return pynini.cross(inp, out)




def apply_fst(fst, text: str):
    """
    Apply FST to text and return the output string.
    Returns None if the input is rejected.
    """
    lattice = pynini.accep(text) @ fst

    try:
        return pynini.shortestpath(lattice).string()
    except pywrapfst.FstOpError:
        return None


# Morphological analyzer:
# running -> run+ing
#
# The second "n" is the doubled consonant.
# It is consumed as input and output as "+".
fst = (
    x("r", "r")
    + x("u", "u")
    + x("n", "n")
    + x("n", "+")
    + x("i", "i")
    + x("n", "n")
    + x("g", "g")
).optimize()

symbols = byte_symbol_table("runig+")

dot_path = Path("fst_running_analyzer.dot")
png_path = Path("fst_running_analyzer.png")

fst.draw(
    str(dot_path),
    portrait=True,
    isymbols=symbols,
    osymbols=symbols,
)

subprocess.run(
    ["dot", "-Tpng", str(dot_path), "-o", str(png_path)],
    check=True,
)

print("running ->", apply_fst(fst, "running"))

display(Image(filename=str(png_path)))
```

```{dot}
//| label: fig-fst-morphology
//| fig-cap: "Transducer for morphological analysis of 'running' -> 'run+ing'"
//| file: fst_morphology.dot
```



```{python}
#| label: fst-morph-epsilon-plus
#| echo: true

from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

import pynini
import pywrapfst
from IPython.display import Image, display


def x(inp: str, out: str):
    return pynini.cross(inp, out)


def eps_out(out: str):
    # epsilon on the input side, symbol on the output side
    return pynini.cross("", out)


def byte_symbol_table(text: str):
    syms = pywrapfst.SymbolTable()
    syms.add_symbol("<eps>", 0)
    for ch in sorted(set(text)):
        syms.add_symbol(ch, ord(ch))
    return syms


def apply_fst(fst, text: str):
    lattice = pynini.accep(text) @ fst
    try:
        return pynini.shortestpath(lattice).string()
    except pywrapfst.FstOpError:
        return None


# ---------- stems ----------
# surface -> lexical stem

run_stem = x("run", "run")
jump_stem = x("jump", "jump")
happy_stem = x("happi", "happy")   # happiness surface stem -> lexical happy


# ---------- suffixes ----------
# boundary is inserted with epsilon:+

ing_regular = eps_out("+") + x("ing", "ing")
ing_after_n = eps_out("+") + x("ning", "ing")   # running: surface ning -> lexical ing
ness = eps_out("+") + x("ness", "ness")


# ---------- whole-word analyzers ----------
running = run_stem + ing_after_n
jumping = jump_stem + ing_regular
happiness = happy_stem + ness

fst = (running | jumping | happiness).optimize()


# ---------- draw ----------
all_symbols = "runningjumpinghappinessrun+ingjump+inghappy+ness"
syms = byte_symbol_table(all_symbols)

dot_path = Path("fst_morphology1.dot")
png_path = Path("fst_morphology1.png")

fst.draw(
    str(dot_path),
    portrait=True,
    isymbols=syms,
    osymbols=syms,
)

subprocess.run(
    ["dot", "-Tpng", str(dot_path), "-o", str(png_path)],
    check=True,
)


# ---------- test ----------
for w in ["running", "jumping", "happiness", "runner"]:
    print(f"{w:10s} -> {apply_fst(fst, w)}")

#display(Image(filename=str(png_path)))
```

```{dot}
//| label: fig-fst-morphology-multiple
//| fig-cap: "Transducer for additional morphological analysis"
//| file: fst_morphology1.dot
```


```{python}

#| label: morph-lexicon-rules-analyzer
#| echo: true

from pathlib import Path
import string
import subprocess

import pandas as pd
import pynini
import pywrapfst
from pynini.lib import pynutil
from IPython.display import Image, display


# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------

def accep(s: str):
    return pynini.accep(s)


def word(prefix: str, stem: str, suffix: str):
    """
    Build lexical forms while omitting empty morphemes.

    Examples:
      word("",   "run",   "")     -> run
      word("un", "happy", "")     -> un+happy
      word("",   "run",   "er")   -> run+er
      word("un", "happy", "ness") -> un+happy+ness
    """
    parts = [part for part in [prefix, stem, suffix] if part != ""]
    return pynini.accep("+".join(parts))


def byte_symbol_table(chars: str):
    """
    Pynini's default ASCII labels are byte integers.
    This table makes Graphviz show letters instead of numbers.
    """
    syms = pywrapfst.SymbolTable()
    syms.add_symbol("<eps>", 0)
    for ch in sorted(set(chars)):
        syms.add_symbol(ch, ord(ch))
    return syms


def one_best(fst, text: str):
    """
    Apply FST to text and return the best output string.
    Returns None if the input is rejected.
    """
    lattice = pynini.compose(accep(text), fst)

    # No valid path.
    if lattice.start() == pywrapfst.NO_STATE_ID:
        return None

    try:
        path = pynini.shortestpath(lattice, nshortest=1, unique=True).optimize()

        # Critical fix:
        # convert the relation into its output string language.
        path.project("output")
        path.rmepsilon()

        return path.string()
    except pywrapfst.FstOpError:
        return None


# ----------------------------
# 1. Lexicon / morphotactics
# ----------------------------

LEXICON = pynini.union(
    word("",   "happy", ""),       # happy+er
    word("un", "happy", ""),       # un+happy
    word("",   "happy", "er"),     # un+happy+er
    word("un", "happy", "er"),     # un+happy+er
    word("",   "happy", "est"),    # happy+est
    word("un", "happy", "est"),    # un+happy+est
    word("",   "happy", "ness"),   # happy+ness
    word("un", "happy", "ness"),   # un+happy+ness
    word("",   "run",   ""),       # run
    word("",   "run",   "er"),     # run+er
    word("",   "run",   "ing"),    # run+ing
    word("re", "run",   "ing"),    # re+run+ing
    word("",   "jump",  ""),       # jump
    word("",   "jump",  "ed"),     # jump+ed
    word("",   "jump",  "ing"),    # jump+ing
    word("",   "jump",  "er"),     # jump+er/N
    word("",   "jump",  "y"),     # jump+y/ADJ
    word("",   "jumpy",  "er"),    # jumpy+ier/ADJ
    word("",   "jumpy",  "est"),   # jumpy+iest/ADJ
).optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 2. Spelling rules
# ----------------------------

ALPHABET = string.ascii_lowercase + "+"
SIGMA_STAR = pynini.union(*ALPHABET).closure().optimize()

PLUS = accep("+")
ING = accep("ing")
NESS = accep("ness")
ER = accep("er")


# happy+ness -> happi+ness
y_to_i_before_suffix = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynini.cross("y", "i"),
    "",
    PLUS + (NESS | ER),
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# run+er -> runn+er
double_n_before_er = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.insert("n"),
    accep("n"),
    PLUS + ER,
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()

# run+ing -> runn+ing
double_n_before_ing = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.insert("n"),
    accep("n"),
    PLUS + ING,
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# jump+ing -> jumping
# re+runn+ing -> rerunning
# un+happi+ness -> unhappiness
delete_boundaries = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.delete("+"),
    "",
    "",
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 3. Generator and analyzer
# ----------------------------

GENERATOR = (
    LEXICON
    @ y_to_i_before_suffix
    @ double_n_before_er
    @ double_n_before_ing
    @ delete_boundaries
).optimize()

ANALYZER = GENERATOR.invert().optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 4. Test lexical -> surface
# ----------------------------

lexical_tests = [
    "run",
    "jump",
    "happy",
    "un+happy",
    "run+er",
    "run+ing",
    "re+run+ing",
    "jump+ing",
    "happy+ness",
    "un+happy+ness",
]

gen_rows = [
    {"lexical": x, "surface": one_best(GENERATOR, x)}
    for x in lexical_tests
]

display(pd.DataFrame(gen_rows))


# ----------------------------
# 5. Test surface -> lexical analysis
# ----------------------------

surface_tests = [
    "happy",
    "unhappy",
    "happier",    
    "unhappier",    
    "happiness",
    "unhappiness",    
    "run",
    "running",
    "rerunning",
    "runner",
    "jump",
    "jumpier",
    "jumpiest",    
    "jumped",
    "jumping",
    "jumper",
]


ana_rows = [
    {"surface": x, "analysis": one_best(ANALYZER, x)}
    for x in surface_tests
]

display(pd.DataFrame(ana_rows))


# ----------------------------
# 6. Draw analyzer as PNG
# ----------------------------

syms = byte_symbol_table(ALPHABET)

dot_path = Path("morph_analyzer2.dot")
png_path = Path("morph_analyzer2.png")

ANALYZER.draw(
    str(dot_path),
    portrait=True,
    isymbols=syms,
    osymbols=syms,
)

subprocess.run(
    ["dot", "-Tpng", str(dot_path), "-o", str(png_path)],
    check=True,
)

#display(Image(filename=str(png_path)))
```

```{dot}
//| label: fig-morph-analyzer
//| fig-cap: "FST for morphological analysis with lexicon and spelling rules"
//| file: morph_analyzer2.dot
```


The above analyzer is good at breaking down surface forms into their underlying lexical morphemes.
However we should get a more detailed analysis of the morphemes, e.g. happy+ness -> happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>
This will not only a more detailed analysis but also simplify generation since we can have a separate FST that maps from the compact feature specifications to the full tagged analyses, and then use the same spelling rules for generation and analysis.

How this sample works:

## Overview

The sample separates **morphological analysis** into two layers. The first layer maps between **surface spellings** and **underlying lexical morphemes**: for example, `happiness` is analyzed as `happy+ness`, and `rerunning` as `re+run+ing`. This captures the hidden morpheme boundaries and reverses spelling alternations such as `y → i` in `happy → happiness` or consonant doubling in `run → running`.

The second layer enriches those morphemes with grammatical information. Instead of stopping at `happy+ness`, the analyzer can return `happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>`, making explicit that `happy` is an adjectival stem and `-ness` is a nominalizing suffix that derives a noun. This richer tagged representation is also useful for generation: a compact feature request such as `happy/ADJ<NMLZ>` can first be expanded into the full tagged analysis, then passed through the same lexical and spelling-rule pipeline that the analyzer uses in reverse. This keeps the system modular: **features choose the morphology; spelling rules realize it.**


```{python}
#| label: tagged-morphology
#| echo: true

from pathlib import Path
import string
import subprocess

import pandas as pd
import pynini
import pywrapfst
from pynini.lib import pynutil
from IPython.display import Image, display


# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------

def accep(s: str):
    return pynini.accep(s)


def one_best(fst, text: str):
    """
    Apply FST to text and return the best output string.
    Returns None if the input is rejected.
    """
    lattice = pynini.compose(accep(text), fst)

    if lattice.start() == pywrapfst.NO_STATE_ID:
        return None

    try:
        path = pynini.shortestpath(lattice, nshortest=1, unique=True).optimize()
        path.project("output")
        path.rmepsilon()
        return path.string()
    except pywrapfst.FstOpError:
        return None


def byte_symbol_table(chars: str):
    syms = pywrapfst.SymbolTable()
    syms.add_symbol("<eps>", 0)
    for ch in sorted(set(chars)):
        syms.add_symbol(ch, ord(ch))
    return syms


# ----------------------------
# 1. Feature specs -> tagged analyses
# ----------------------------

FEATURE_TO_ANALYSIS = pynini.string_map([
    # happy adjective family
    ("happy/ADJ",              "happy/STEM<ADJ>"),
    ("happy/ADJ<NEG>",         "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>"),
    ("happy/ADJ<COMP>",        "happy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP"),
    ("happy/ADJ<NEG><COMP>",   "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP"),
    ("happy/ADJ<SUP>",         "happy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP"),
    ("happy/ADJ<NEG><SUP>",    "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP"),
    ("happy/ADJ<NMLZ>",        "happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>"),
    ("happy/ADJ<NEG><NMLZ>",   "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>"),

    # run verb family
    ("run/VERB",               "run/STEM<VERB>"),
    ("run/VERB<AGENT>",        "run/STEM<VERB>+er/AGENT<N>"),
    ("run/VERB<PROG>",         "run/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG"),
    ("run/VERB<RE><PROG>",     "re/PREFIX+run/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG"),

    # jump verb / adjective family
    ("jump/VERB",              "jump/STEM<VERB>"),
    ("jump/VERB<PAST>",        "jump/STEM<VERB>+ed/PAST"),
    ("jump/VERB<PROG>",        "jump/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG"),
    ("jump/VERB<AGENT>",       "jump/STEM<VERB>+er/AGENT<N>"),
    ("jump/VERB<ADJZ>",        "jump/STEM<VERB>+y/ADJZ<ADJ>"),

    # derived adjective jumpy
    ("jumpy/ADJ",              "jumpy/STEM<ADJ>"),
    ("jumpy/ADJ<COMP>",        "jumpy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP"),
    ("jumpy/ADJ<SUP>",         "jumpy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP"),
]).optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 2. Tagged analyses -> underlying lexical forms
# ----------------------------

ANALYSIS_TO_LEXICAL = pynini.string_map([
    # happy adjective family
    ("happy/STEM<ADJ>",                         "happy"),
    ("un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>",                  "un+happy"),
    ("happy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP",                 "happy+er"),
    ("un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP",          "un+happy+er"),
    ("happy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP",                 "happy+est"),
    ("un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP",          "un+happy+est"),
    ("happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>",            "happy+ness"),
    ("un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>",     "un+happy+ness"),

    # run family
    ("run/STEM<VERB>",                          "run"),
    ("run/STEM<VERB>+er/AGENT<N>",              "run+er"),
    ("run/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG",                 "run+ing"),
    ("re/PREFIX+run/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG",       "re+run+ing"),

    # jump family
    ("jump/STEM<VERB>",                         "jump"),
    ("jump/STEM<VERB>+ed/PAST",                 "jump+ed"),
    ("jump/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG",                "jump+ing"),
    ("jump/STEM<VERB>+er/AGENT<N>",             "jump+er"),
    ("jump/STEM<VERB>+y/ADJZ<ADJ>",             "jump+y"),

    # jumpy adjective family
    ("jumpy/STEM<ADJ>",                         "jumpy"),
    ("jumpy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP",                 "jumpy+er"),
    ("jumpy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP",                 "jumpy+est"),
]).optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 3. Spelling rules
# ----------------------------

ALPHABET = string.ascii_lowercase + "+"
SIGMA_STAR = pynini.union(*ALPHABET).closure().optimize()

PLUS = accep("+")
ER = accep("er")
EST = accep("est")
ING = accep("ing")
NESS = accep("ness")


# happy+er    -> happi+er
# happy+est   -> happi+est
# happy+ness  -> happi+ness
# jumpy+er    -> jumpi+er
# jumpy+est   -> jumpi+est
y_to_i_before_suffix = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynini.cross("y", "i"),
    "",
    PLUS + (ER | EST | NESS),
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# run+er  -> runn+er
double_n_before_er = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.insert("n"),
    accep("n"),
    PLUS + ER,
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# run+ing -> runn+ing
double_n_before_ing = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.insert("n"),
    accep("n"),
    PLUS + ING,
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


# delete lexical morpheme boundaries
delete_boundaries = pynini.cdrewrite(
    pynutil.delete("+"),
    "",
    "",
    SIGMA_STAR,
).optimize()


SPELLING = (
    y_to_i_before_suffix
    @ double_n_before_er
    @ double_n_before_ing
    @ delete_boundaries
).optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 4. Generators and analyzer
# ----------------------------

# Full tagged analysis -> surface
GENERATOR = (
    ANALYSIS_TO_LEXICAL
    @ SPELLING
).optimize()

# Compact feature spec -> surface
FEATURE_GENERATOR = (
    FEATURE_TO_ANALYSIS
    @ GENERATOR
).optimize()

# Surface -> full tagged analysis
ANALYZER = GENERATOR.invert().optimize()


# ----------------------------
# 5. Test feature spec -> surface
# ----------------------------

feature_tests = [
    "happy/ADJ",
    "happy/ADJ<NEG>",
    "happy/ADJ<COMP>",
    "happy/ADJ<NEG><COMP>",
    "happy/ADJ<SUP>",
    "happy/ADJ<NEG><SUP>",
    "happy/ADJ<NMLZ>",
    "happy/ADJ<NEG><NMLZ>",

    "run/VERB",
    "run/VERB<AGENT>",
    "run/VERB<PROG>",
    "run/VERB<RE><PROG>",

    "jump/VERB",
    "jump/VERB<PAST>",
    "jump/VERB<PROG>",
    "jump/VERB<AGENT>",
    "jump/VERB<ADJZ>",

    "jumpy/ADJ",
    "jumpy/ADJ<COMP>",
    "jumpy/ADJ<SUP>",
]

feature_rows = [
    {"feature_spec": x, "surface": one_best(FEATURE_GENERATOR, x)}
    for x in feature_tests
]

display(pd.DataFrame(feature_rows))


# ----------------------------
# 6. Test tagged analysis -> surface
# ----------------------------

analysis_tests = [
    "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+ness/NMLZ<N>",
    "un/NEG+happy/STEM<ADJ>+er/COMP",
    "re/PREFIX+run/STEM<VERB>+ing/PROG",
    "jump/STEM<VERB>+y/ADJZ<ADJ>",
    "jumpy/STEM<ADJ>+est/SUP",
]

analysis_rows = [
    {"analysis": x, "surface": one_best(GENERATOR, x)}
    for x in analysis_tests
]

display(pd.DataFrame(analysis_rows))


# ----------------------------
# 7. Test surface -> tagged analysis
# ----------------------------

surface_tests = [
    "happy",
    "unhappy",
    "happier",
    "unhappier",
    "happiest",
    "unhappiest",
    "happiness",
    "unhappiness",

    "run",
    "runner",
    "running",
    "rerunning",

    "jump",
    "jumped",
    "jumping",
    "jumper",
    "jumpy",
    "jumpier",
    "jumpiest",
]

surface_rows = [
    {"surface": x, "analysis": one_best(ANALYZER, x)}
    for x in surface_tests
]

display(pd.DataFrame(surface_rows))


# ----------------------------
# 8. Draw analyzer
# ----------------------------

draw_chars = (
    string.ascii_lowercase
    + "+/<>"
    + "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ"
)

syms = byte_symbol_table(draw_chars)

dot_path = Path("tagged_morph_analyzer.dot")
png_path = Path("tagged_morph_analyzer.png")

ANALYZER.draw(
    str(dot_path),
    portrait=True,
    isymbols=syms,
    osymbols=syms,
)

subprocess.run(
    ["dot", "-Tpng", str(dot_path), "-o", str(png_path)],
    check=True,
)

display(Image(filename=str(png_path)))
```
